{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 40,

  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Brazing performed without nitrogen purge resulting in black scale (copper oxide) inside tubing",
    "Contaminated piping system will cause compressor damage, TXV blockage, and filter-drier fouling",
    "Sequence violation: nitrogen purge is mandatory during brazing per manufacturer specs and ACCA Standard 5"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop all brazing work immediately",
    "Cut out all affected piping sections that show internal oxidation",
    "Replace contaminated components including filter-drier and any affected valves",
    "Re-braze with continuous nitrogen purge flowing at 1-3 psig to prevent oxidation",
    "Perform proper evacuation to below 500 microns with decay test before charging"
  ],
  "rationale": "Brazing without nitrogen purge creates black copper oxide scale that contaminates the refrigerant circuit. This is a known cause of premature compressor failure and metering device blockage. The work cannot proceed on the existing contaminated piping; it must be removed and replaced with properly purged connections per manufacturer installation instructions, ACCA Standard 5 (QI 5.3), and EPA 608 safe handling practices for system integrity.",
  "references": [
    "Manufacturer installation instructions (brazing section - nitrogen purge requirement)",
    "ACCA Standard 5: HVAC Quality Installation Specification (5.3 - System Integrity - Brazing Procedures)",
    "EPA 608 (refrigerant handling and system contamination prevention)"
  ]
}
